$comment: Normative appendix for L1-SG-01; extracted from protocols/algedonic.md (HALT/ALERT payload template and category classes) and skills/pact-agent-teams/SKILL.md (blocker report shape) at plugin 4.6.8 / commit b4041ccf. Normatively stronger than the as-built realization, which enforced no payload validator at the fork point; conformance annexes record this as satisfied-with-deviation.
title: Signal payload
description: A blocker, ALERT, or HALT payload. Shape-only: bypass, scope-amplification, and resolution semantics live in the prose requirements L1-SG-01 through L1-SG-04.

level.description: Severity class. HALT and ALERT are Principal-directed; blocker is coordinator-triaged.

category.description: Signal category; the legal set is closed per level (see the oneOf branches).

partial_handoff.description: Partial completion state accompanying the signal. A complete instance validates against the handoff schema (urn:pact:spec:0.1.0:handoff.schema.json); partial instances are not required to.

oneOf.description: HALT — viability threat; blocks feature-scope work.
ALERT — serious degradation; blocks phase-scope work.

examples.issue: Provider API key committed in a configuration fixture
examples.evidence: config/fixtures/provider.json line 12 contains a live-format key
examples.impact: Key is exposed to anyone with repository read access
examples.recommended_action: Rotate the key, purge the fixture, and gate fixtures on a secret scan

examples.description: The ledger service's reversal-entry contract is undocumented and its owner is unavailable; cannot define the worker interface without it.

produced: services/payments/refund_worker: skeleton and tests for the idempotency key
decisions: Deferred interface definition pending the ledger contract
open_questions: Who owns the ledger reversal contract?
